title: rbac — resolver + cache driver specifications
description: RBAC has two driver-shaped extension points: (a) the `PermissionResolver` composes a chain of ResolverStrategyInterface implementations at runtime; (b) the `PermissionCacheManager` writes through a CacheDriverInterface. Both are set-once bindings on the container — there is NO tenant-configurable driver override (unlike localization's translator drivers). This file exists to document the extension surface for future modules that legitimately need to plug in an alternate resolver strategy (e.g. an OPA-backed strategy in Wave 3 when we consider policy-as-code).

resolver_strategy.purpose: One step of the composite resolver algorithm. Called in a fixed order by PermissionResolver::resolve().

methods.returns: ResolveOutcome DTO: decision (allow / deny / abstain), source (rbac / grant / delegation / default), matched_permissions (array), diagnostics (array for the audit-permission-check command).

methods.description: Order in the chain. Lower runs first. Framework strategies occupy 1–19: DenyGrantStrategy=1, AllowGrantStrategy=5, RbacRoleStrategy=10, RbacDirectPermissionStrategy=12, DelegationStrategy=15.

cache_driver.purpose: The write-through cache for ResolvedPermissionSet. Set-once at boot from config('rbac.cache.store').

framework_strategies.name: DenyGrantStrategy
framework_strategies.purpose: Step 1 of the algorithm: explicit DENY grants win. Consults `access/grants::GrantResolverInterface`. Returns `deny` on match; `abstain` otherwise.
framework_strategies.graceful_degradation: When access/grants is not booted (running RBAC in isolation for tests), the strategy is skipped.

framework_strategies.name: AllowGrantStrategy
framework_strategies.purpose: Step 2: explicit ALLOW grants. Same consultation as DenyGrantStrategy but returns `allow` on match.

framework_strategies.name: RbacRoleStrategy
framework_strategies.purpose: Step 3a: walk `model_has_roles` to enumerate the principal's roles, then walk `role_has_permissions` to enumerate the permissions. THE hot path — optimised for the resolved-permission cache.

framework_strategies.name: RbacDirectPermissionStrategy
framework_strategies.purpose: Step 3b: `model_has_permissions` direct grants (rare — usually roles carry perms). Merged with role-walked permissions.

framework_strategies.name: DelegationStrategy
framework_strategies.purpose: Step 4: if the principal is a delegate for the current tenant + role, evaluate as the delegator. Recursion-safe (max delegation depth 3 to guard against pathological chains).

extension_note: New resolver strategies can be registered via `PermissionResolver::registerStrategy(ResolverStrategyInterface)`. Third-party strategies must not modify the framework strategies' priorities (1–19 is reserved). Domain modules that need custom permission-inference logic should register at priority ≥100.
